name: Pickled Beet and Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off all but 2-inches of the beet tops, leaving the root ends attached.
2. Peel and slice the onions into 1/4-slices.
3. Heat enough water to cover beets to boiling.
4. Add beets and 2 tablespoons vinegar for each quart of water used.
5. Cover and bring to a boil. Cook until beets are tender, 35 to 45 minutes.
6. Drain the beets. Run cold water over them, slip off skins and remove root ends.
7. Cut beets into 1/4-inch slices.
8. Heat remaining ingredients to boiling in a 6-quart Dutch oven.
9. Reduce heat. Simmer, uncovered for 10 minutes.
10. Stir in beets.
11. Pack beets and onions into hot jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
12. Bring syrup to a boil.
13. Pour over beets and onions, leaving about 1/2-inch headspace.
14. Seal the jars.
15. Process for 30 minutes in a boiling water bath.
Makes about 8 Pints.
NOTE:
7 cans (16 ounces each) sliced beets, drained, can be substituted for the beets in the above recipe.
